description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a set of dark photovoltaic panels mounted on a rectangular base. The panels are arranged in a stepped, tiered configuration, likely to maximize sunlight exposure and efficiency. The base appears to be constructed from a sturdy, light-colored material, possibly metal or reinforced concrete, providing stability and housing for electrical components. On one end of the base, there are small protruding elements, possibly representing electrical inverters or connection terminals, and a few visible control or monitoring units. The overall design is low-profile and modular, suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility spaces. The structure is engineered for durability and efficient energy capture, with minimal visible cabling and a clean, industrial aesthetic.
description: A compact, stepped solar panel array on a sturdy rectangular base with visible electrical components and a clean, industrial design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the main base, with electrical components visible on the left.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the stepped arrangement of the panels and the protruding electrical units at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the base and the electrical components, with the panel array visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the tiered structure of the panels and the compactness of the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum) frame and reinforced base
